summary: Descriptive overview of privacytorts in substantive law: intrusion, appropriation, false light, public disclosure.
key_points: intrusion is a fundamental concept in tort law.
Legal standards for privacytorts are established through case law and statutory interpretation.
Courts apply specific tests to determine privacytorts in litigation.
The doctrine of privacytorts has evolved through Supreme Court precedents.
Understanding privacytorts requires analysis of both historical context and modern applications.
philosophy_guard_statement: This fragment describes existing legal doctrines and their applications without advocating for normative changes.
